title: dianthus bucovinensis
intro: Dianthus bucovinensis, also known as the Bucovinian pink or mountain pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to the Carpathian Mountains in Romania and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Dianthus bucovinensis can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, with the leaves and flowers being used for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
